pattern: Working backwards from the target and using modulo to collapse long chains of repeated subtraction turns an exponential forward search into a near-logarithmic walk.
hints: Searching forward branches in two directions and explodes; the target is reached far more directly by undoing moves from the end.
From a point the only previous state is obtained by subtracting the smaller coordinate from the larger, and many such subtractions in a row can be done at once with a remainder.

While both target coordinates exceed the start, reduce the larger one modulo the smaller; once one coordinate matches its start, check whether the other can be reached by subtracting whole multiples of the fixed start coordinate.

edge_inputs.description: reachable via a mix of both move types
target looks close but is unreachable
one axis fixed, reachable by repeated addition
one axis fixed but the other is not a reachable multiple
single straight chain on the x axis
target smaller than start cannot be reached
large coprime start reduces via modulo

stress.description: construct reachable targets by replaying a bounded number of random forward moves so the answer is genuinely true
stress.note: bounded moves keep targets small so the one-step-back oracle stays fast while remaining reachable

stress.description: small random quadruples that are usually unreachable so the bounded oracle stays fast while disagreeing-prone cases are covered
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    return [rng.randint(1, 40), rng.randint(1, 40), rng.randint(1, 400), rng.randint(1, 400)]
stress.note: small coordinates keep the one-step-back oracle tractable
